title: cerastium arvense subsp. calcicola
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. calcicola, also known as field chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium arvense plant, which belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and health-promoting properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Cerastium arvense subsp. calcicola include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to address various health concerns.
